summary: Iter20 backend tests — 9/9 passed. Verified: (1) spin blocked for users under $50 lifetime deposits with correct amount_needed math; (2) 403 with 'need at least $50' on POST /api/spin/spin; (3) eligible user (testbugfix1, history cleared) successfully spins, prize in [1,2,3,4,5,6,40], jackpot bool consistent, next_spin_days=7; (4) immediate re-spin returns 429 'Come back in N day(s)' and status shows can_spin=false, days_left>=1; (5) prizes schema equals [1,2,3,4,5,6,40]; (6) GET /api/5sim/services (no auth) returns exactly ['whatsapp','signal','viber','tiktok','telegram'] with price+name+icon+default_country+default_operator; (7) admin POST /api/admin/5sim/config accepts custom prices+api_key, GET masks api_key='***' with preview containing '…' and returns products list, and public services reflect the new prices; (8) POST /api/5sim/buy with product='amazon' → 400 mentioning supported products; (9) POST /api/5sim/buy with product='whatsapp' as low-balance user (after admin bumps price to $9999.99) → 400 'Not enough balance' before any 5sim network call; (10) regression: /api/chat/tip never returns 429 across 3 rapid calls (iter19 fix holds).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 3851 lines — should be split into feature modules (spin, 5sim, funds, tickets, etc.) for maintainability.
5sim admin config endpoint accepts arbitrary api_key over HTTP header-guarded route; consider logging when the key changes (audit trail).
spin_status computes days_left as int(gap/86400) which for gap just under 7 days rounds down; edge case at exactly 7 days works (days_left = max(0, 7-6)=1) but tests confirm cooldown fires.
test_credentials: testbugfix1/password1 (used for eligible-spin + tip regression); ephemeral nospintest_<rand>/password1 (created via /api/auth/register with math captcha, $10 seed deposit inserted directly in mongo); admin token via POST /api/admin/login-secret {secret:'haha123'}.
seed_data_creation: Test fixture creates a fresh 'nospintest_<rand>' user with a $10 approved deposit via db.transactions.insert_one; cleanup deletes user + their transactions + spin_wheel rows on teardown. testbugfix1's spin_wheel history is cleared before the eligible-spin test; a spin_prize transaction may remain (also cleared prior). 5sim admin config is left with normal prices at the end (restored in finally).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 9 spin+5sim+regression checks pass. Note: /api/auth/me returns {user: {...}} not the user object directly — accessing r.json()['user']['id']. testbugfix1 has ~$70 lifetime deposits (eligible for spin). The 5sim /buy endpoint does local validation (product whitelist → config check → balance check) before any HTTPS call to 5sim.net, so tests can safely exercise the 400 paths without a real 5sim API key.
